notes: OpenSSL is a critical internet dependency — used by nearly every web server (Apache, nginx), programming language (Python, Ruby, PHP), and application (curl, git, databases). An OpenSSL vulnerability can affect nearly every internet-connected system. Notable: CVE-2014-0160 (Heartbleed), CVE-2014-0224 (CCS Injection), CVE-2022-3602 (X.509 buffer overflow). OpenSSL 3.x is current; 1.1.1 is EOL. LibreSSL and BoringSSL are forks. Project moved to openssl-library.org.

data.description: Security advisories published by the OpenSSL project, organized by date. Each advisory may cover multiple CVEs.
data.notes: OpenSSL groups multiple CVEs into single dated advisory files. Advisory URLs use YYYYMMDD date format. The project moved from openssl.org to openssl-library.org; legacy URLs at https://www.openssl.org/news/secadv/ still work.

data.note: OpenSSL groups multiple CVEs into single dated advisory files. A CVE alone cannot determine which advisory file contains it. Use the date-based lookup (YYYYMMDD) for direct resolution, or search the advisory listing page.

examples.note: Heartbleed — advisory date 20140407, but CVE alone doesn't resolve to URL
